args.name: MateTypeFromEnum
args.doc: Type of mate as defined in swMateType_e (see Remarks)

args.name: AlignFromEnum
args.doc: Type of alignment as defined in swMateAlign_e

args.name: Flip
args.doc: True to flip the mate entities, false to not; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMatetype_e.swMateDISTANCE

args.name: Distance
args.doc: Distance value; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMateType_e.swMateDISTANCE

args.name: DistanceAbsUpperLimit
args.doc: Absolute maximum distance value; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMateType_e.swMateDISTANCE (see Remarks)

args.name: DistanceAbsLowerLimit
args.doc: Absolute minimum distance value; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMateType_e.swMateDISTANCE (see Remarks)

args.name: GearRatioNumerator
args.doc: Gear ratio numerator value; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMateType_e.swMateGEAR

args.name: GearRatioDenominator
args.doc: Gear ratio denominator value; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMateType_e.swMateGEAR

args.name: Angle
args.doc: Angle value; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMateType_e.swMateANGLE

args.name: AngleAbsUpperLimit
args.doc: Absolute maximum angle value; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMateType_e.swMateANGLE

args.name: AngleAbsLowerLimit
args.doc: Absolute minimum angle value; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMateType_e.swMateANGLE

args.name: ForPositioningOnly
args.doc: True to only position the components according to the mating relationship and not return a mate, false to return a mate

args.name: LockRotation
args.doc: True to lock component rotation, false to not

args.name: WidthMateOption
args.doc: Width mate options as defined in swMateWidthOptions_e; valid only if MateTypeFromEnum is swMateType_e.swMateWIDTH

args.name: ErrorStatus
args.doc: Success or error as defined by swAddMateError_e

IEquationMgr.Add2.summary: Adds an equation at the specified index.

args.name: Index
args.doc: 0-based index of the new equation (-1 places it at the end of the list)

args.name: Equation
args.doc: String containing the equation (see Remarks)

args.name: Solve
args.doc: True to solve the equation immediately; false otherwise (see Remarks)

IFeature.GetNextFeature.summary: Gets the next feature in the part.

IFeature.GetTypeName.summary: Gets the type of feature. NOTE: To get the underlying type of feature of an Instant3D feature (i.e., "ICE"), call this method; otherwise, call IFeature::GetTypeName2.

IFeatureManager.CreateDefinition.summary: Creates a feature data object of the specified type.

args.name: Type
args.doc: Feature name ID as defined in swFeatureNameID_e: swFMBaseFlange (sheet metal base flange) swFMBeltAndChain (belt/chain) swFmBoundingBox (bounding box) swFmCirPattern (circular pattern) swFmCornerRelief (sheet metal corner relief) swFmCurvePattern (curve-driven pattern) swFmDerivedLPattern (derived-driven pattern) swFmDimPattern (variable/dimension pattern) swFmEdgeFlange (sheet metal edge flange) swFmFillet (constant radius, face, full round fillet/chamfer) swFmFillPattern (fill pattern) swFmGroundPlane (ground plane) swFmLibraryFeature (library) swFmLocalChainPattern (chain component pattern) swFmLocalCirPattern (circular component pattern) swFmLocalCurvePattern (curve-driven component pattern) swFmLocalLPattern (linear component pattern) swFmLocalSketchPattern (sketch-driven component pattern) swFmLPattern (linear pattern) swFmMateController (mate controller) swFmMirrorComponent (mirror components) swFmNormalCut (sheet metal normal cut) swFmRefCurve (projection curve) swFmRefSurface (surface sweep) swFmSketchBend (sheet metal sketched bend) swFmSketchPattern (sketch-driven pattern) swFmSMGusset (sheet metal gusset) swFmSweep (boss sweep) swFmSweepCut (cut sweep) swFmSweepThread (sweep thread) swFmSweptFlange (sheet metal swept flange) swFmTabAndSlot (tab and slot) swFmTablePattern (table pattern)

args.name: FeatureData
args.doc: thread, sweep, library, tab/slot, bounding box, ground plane, mirror components, projection curve, sheet metal normal cut, sheet metal swept flange, sheet metal gusset, sheet metal edge flange, simple fillet/chamfer, belt/chain, sheet metal base flange, sheet metal corner relief, sheet metal sketched bend, mate controller, or a pattern-specific feature data object (see Remarks)

IFeatureManager.FeatureCircularPattern5.summary: Obsolete. See IFeatureManager::CreateFeature and the Remarks in ICircularPatternFeatureData and ILocalCircularPatternFeatureData.

args.name: Number
args.doc: Number of instances of the circular pattern to insert in Direction 1, including the original instance

args.name: Spacing
args.doc: Spacing between each instance in Direction 1 of the circular pattern or, if EqualSpacing is true, then the total angle in radians

args.name: FlipDirection
args.doc: True to flip the direction of the circular pattern in Direction 1, false to not

args.name: DName
args.doc: Name of the angular dimension defining Direction 1 of the pattern

args.name: GeometryPattern
args.doc: True to use geometry pattern, false to not

args.name: EqualSpacing
args.doc: True to use equal spacing in Direction 1, false to not

args.name: VaryInstance
args.doc: True to vary the dimensions or spacing of individual pattern instances, false to not; valid only if GeometryPattern = false (see Remarks)

args.name: SyncSubAssemblies
args.doc: True to move components in the patterned instances when components are moved in the seed flexible subassembly, false to not

args.name: BDir2
args.doc: True to create a bidirectional circular pattern feature, false to not

args.name: BSymmetric
args.doc: True to create a symmetric circular pattern feature in Direction 2, false to create an asymmetrical circular pattern feature in Direction 2; valid only if BDir2 is true

args.name: Number2
args.doc: Number of instances to insert in Direction 2; valid only if BDir2 is true

args.name: Spacing2
args.doc: Distance between pattern instances in Direction 2; valid only if BDir2 is true

args.name: DName2
args.doc: Name of the angular dimension defining Direction 2 of the pattern; valid only if BDir2 is true

args.name: EqualSpacing2
args.doc: True to use equal spacing in Direction 2, false to not; valid only if BDir2 is true and BSymmetric is false

args.name: Sd
args.doc: True for a single-ended cut, false for a doubled-ended cut

args.name: Flip
args.doc: True to remove material outside of the profile of the flip side to cut, false to not

args.name: Dir
args.doc: True for Direction 1 to be opposite of the default direction (see Remarks)

args.name: T1
args.doc: Termination type for the first end as defined in swEndConditions_e

args.name: T2
args.doc: Termination type for the second end as defined in swEndConditions_e

args.name: D1
args.doc: Depth of extrusion for the first end in meters

args.name: D2
args.doc: Depth of extrusion for the second end in meters

args.name: Dchk1
args.doc: True allows a draft angle in the first direction, false does not allow drafting in the first direction

args.name: Dchk2
args.doc: True allows a draft angle in the second direction, false does not allow drafting in the second direction

args.name: Ddir1
args.doc: True for the first draft angle to be inward, false to be outward; only valid when Dchk1 is true

args.name: Ddir2
args.doc: True for the second draft angle to be inward, false to be outward; only valid when Dchk2 is true

args.name: Dang1
args.doc: Draft angle for the first end; only valid when Dchk1 is true

args.name: Dang2
args.doc: Draft angle for the second end; only valid when Dchk2 is true

args.name: OffsetReverse1
args.doc: If you chose to offset the first end condition from another face or plane, then true specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in a direction toward the sketch

args.name: OffsetReverse2
args.doc: If you chose to offset the second end condition from another face or plane, then true specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in a direction toward the sketch

args.name: TranslateSurface1
args.doc: When you choose swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface as the termination type for the first end, then true specifies that the end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false specifies to use a true offset

args.name: TranslateSurface2
args.doc: When you choose swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface as the termination type for the second end, then true specifies that the end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false specifies to use a true offset

args.name: NormalCut
args.doc: True to create the cut normal to the sheet metal thickness, false to not (only valid for sheet metal parts; use false for non-sheet metal parts)

args.name: UseFeatScope
args.doc: True if the feature only affects selected bodies or components, false if the feature affects all bodies or components

args.name: UseAutoSelect
args.doc: True to automatically select all bodies or components and have the feature affect those bodies or components, false to only select the bodies or components the feature affects (see Remarks)

args.name: AssemblyFeatureScope
args.doc: True if the assembly feature only affects selected components in the assembly, false if the assembly feature affects all components in the assembly

args.name: AutoSelectComponents
args.doc: True to automatically select all affected components, false to use only the selected components

args.name: PropagateFeatureToParts
args.doc: True to propagate the assembly feature to the components in the model that it affects, false to not

args.name: T0
args.doc: Start conditions as defined in swStartConditions_e

args.name: StartOffset
args.doc: If T0 is swStartConditions_e.swStartOffset, then specify an offset value

args.name: FlipStartOffset
args.doc: If T0 is swStartConditions_e.swStartOffset, then true to flip the direction of cut, false to not

args.name: OptimizeGeometry
args.doc: True to optimize the normal cut in a sheet metal part, false to not; only valid for sheet metal parts and when NormalCut is true

args.name: Sd
args.doc: True for single ended, false for double ended

args.name: Flip
args.doc: True to flip side to cut

args.name: Dir
args.doc: True to flip the direction to extrude

args.name: T1
args.doc: Termination type for first end as defined in swEndConditions_e

args.name: T2
args.doc: Termination type for second end as defined in swEndConditions_e

args.name: D1
args.doc: Depth of extrusion for first end in meters

args.name: D2
args.doc: Depth of extrusion for second end in meters

args.name: Dchk1
args.doc: True allows draft angle in first direction, false does not allow drafting

args.name: Dchk2
args.doc: True allows draft angle in second direction, false does not allow drafting

args.name: Ddir1
args.doc: True for first draft angle to be inward, false to be outward

args.name: Ddir2
args.doc: True for second draft angle to be inward, false to be outward

args.name: Dang1
args.doc: Draft angle for first end

args.name: Dang2
args.doc: Draft angle for second end

args.name: OffsetReverse1
args.doc: If you chose to offset the first end condition from another face or plane, then True specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in direction toward the sketch

args.name: OffsetReverse2
args.doc: If you chose to offset the second end condition from another face or plane, then True specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in direction toward the sketch

args.name: TranslateSurface1
args.doc: When you choose swEndcondOffsetFromSurface as the termination type for the first end, then True specifies that the end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false specifies to use a true offset

args.name: TranslateSurface2
args.doc: When you choose swEndcondOffsetFromSurface as the termination type for the second end, then True specifies that the end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false specifies to use a true offset

args.name: Merge
args.doc: True to merge the results in a multibody part, false to not

args.name: UseFeatScope
args.doc: True if the feature only affects selected bodies, false if the feature affects all bodies

args.name: UseAutoSelect
args.doc: True to automatically select all bodies and have the feature affect those bodies, false to select the bodies the feature affects (see Remarks)

args.name: T0
args.doc: Start condition as defined in swStartConditions_e

args.name: StartOffset
args.doc: If t0 set to swStartOffset, then specify offset value

args.name: FlipStartOffset
args.doc: If t0 set to swStartOffset, then True to flip the direction or false to not

args.name: Sd
args.doc: True for single ended, false for double ended

args.name: Flip
args.doc: True to flip the side to cut

args.name: Dir
args.doc: True to flip the direction of extrusion

args.name: T1
args.doc: Termination type for first end of the extrusion as defined in swEndConditions_e

args.name: T2
args.doc: Termination type for second end of the extrusion as defined in swEndConditions_e

args.name: D1
args.doc: Depth of extrusion for first end in meters; offset, if T1 is set to swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: D2
args.doc: Depth of extrusion for second end in meters; offset, if T2 is set to swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: Dchk1
args.doc: True to allow drafting in the first direction, false to not

args.name: Dchk2
args.doc: True to allow drafting in the second direction, false to not

args.name: Ddir1
args.doc: True for first draft angle to be inward, false to be outward; valid only if Dchk1 is true

args.name: Ddir2
args.doc: True for second draft angle to be inward, false to be outward; valid only if Dchk2 is true

args.name: Dang1
args.doc: Draft angle for first end; valid only if Dchk1 is true

args.name: Dang2
args.doc: Draft angle for second end; valid only if Dchk2 is true

args.name: OffsetReverse1
args.doc: True to offset the first end from another face or plane in a direction away from the sketch, false to offset in a direction toward the sketch; valid only if T1 is set to swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: OffsetReverse2
args.doc: True to offset the second end from another face or plane in a direction away from the sketch, false to offset in a direction toward the sketch; valid only if T2 is set to swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: TranslateSurface1
args.doc: True if the first end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false if it has a true offset; valid only if T1 is set to swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: TranslateSurface2
args.doc: True if the second end of the extrusion is a translation of the reference surface, false if it has a true offset; valid only if T2 is set to swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: Merge
args.doc: True to merge the results in a multibody part, false to not

args.name: UseFeatScope
args.doc: True if the feature only affects selected bodies, false if the feature affects all bodies (see Remarks)

args.name: UseAutoSelect
args.doc: True to automatically select all bodies and have the feature affect those bodies, false to select the bodies that the feature affects (see Remarks)

args.name: T0
args.doc: Start condition as defined in swStartConditions_e

args.name: StartOffset
args.doc: Distance from the sketch plane to start the extrude; valid only if T0 is set to swStartConditions_e.swStartOffset

args.name: FlipStartOffset
args.doc: True to flip the direction of the start offset, false to not; valid only if T0 is set to swStartConditions_e.swStartOffset

IFeatureManager.FeatureLinearPattern5.summary: Obsolete. See IFeatureManager::CreateFeature and the Remarks in ILinearPatternFeatureData and ILocalLinearPatternFeatureData.

args.name: Num1
args.doc: Number of instances of the linear pattern in Direction 1, including the original

args.name: Spacing1
args.doc: Spacing in meters between each instance of the linear pattern in Direction 1

args.name: Num2
args.doc: Number of instances of the linear pattern in Direction 2, including the original

args.name: Spacing2
args.doc: Spacing in meters between each instance of the linear pattern in Direction 2

args.name: FlipDir1
args.doc: True to reverse the direction of the linear pattern in Direction 1, false to not

args.name: FlipDir2
args.doc: True to reverse the direction of the linear pattern in Direction 2, false to not

args.name: DName1
args.doc: Name of the dimension defining Direction 1

args.name: DName2
args.doc: Name of the dimension defining Direction 2

args.name: GeometryPattern
args.doc: True to use geometry pattern, false to not

args.name: VaryInstance
args.doc: True to vary the dimensions and spacing of individual pattern instances, false to not; valid only if GeometryPattern = false (see Remarks)

args.name: HasOffset1
args.doc: True if using Offset1 to specify an offset of the pattern seed from a selected reference in Direction 1, false if not

args.name: HasOffset2
args.doc: True if using Offset2 to specify an offset of the pattern seed from a selected reference in Direction 2, false if not

args.name: CtrlByNum1
args.doc: True to control pattern spacing using Num1, false to control it using Spacing1; valid only if HasOffset1 is true

args.name: CtrlByNum2
args.doc: True to control pattern spacing using Num2, false to control it using Spacing2; valid only if HasOffset2 is true

args.name: FromCentroid1
args.doc: True if Offset1 is measured from the centroid of the seed instance, false if it is measured from a selected reference on the seed instance; valid only if HasOffset1 is true (see Remarks)

args.name: FromCentroid2
args.doc: True if Offset2 is measured from the centroid of the seed instance, false if it is measured from a selected reference on the seed instance; valid only if HasOffset2 is true (see Remarks)

args.name: RevOffset1
args.doc: True to reverse the direction of Offset1, false to not; valid only if HasOffset1 is true

args.name: RevOffset2
args.doc: True to reverse the direction of Offset2, false to not; valid only if HasOffset2 is true

args.name: Offset1
args.doc: Offset in meters from a selected reference in Direction 1; valid only if HasOffset1 is true (see Remarks)

args.name: Offset2
args.doc: Offset in meters from a selected reference in Direction 2; valid only if HasOffset2 is true (see Remarks)

args.name: D2PatternSeedOnly
args.doc: True to create a linear pattern in Direction 2 using the seed features only, without replicating the pattern instances of Direction 1; false to not

args.name: SyncSubAssemblies
args.doc: True to move components in the patterned instances when components are moved in the seed flexible subassembly, false to not

IFeatureManager.FeatureRevolve2.summary: Creates a base-, boss-, or cut-revolve feature.

args.name: SingleDir
args.doc: True if the revolve is in one direction, false if in two directions (see Remarks)

args.name: IsSolid
args.doc: True if this is a solid revolve feature, false if not

args.name: IsThin
args.doc: True if this is a thin revolve feature, false if not

args.name: IsCut
args.doc: True if this is a cut revolve feature, false if not

args.name: ReverseDir
args.doc: True reverses the angle of the revolution, false does not; only applies if Dir1Type is not swEndConditions_e.swEndCondMidPlane

args.name: BothDirectionUpToSameEntity
args.doc: True if the revolve is up to the same entity in both directions, false if not; only applies if SingleDir is false and Dir1Type and Dir2Type are swEndConditions_e.swEndCondUpToVertex, swEndConditions_e.swEndCondUpToSurface, or swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface) (see Remarks)

args.name: Dir1Type
args.doc: Revolve end condition as defined in swEndConditions_e

args.name: Dir2Type
args.doc: Revolve end condition in direction 2; as defined in swEndConditions_e and only applies if Dir1Type is not swEndConditions_e.swEndCondMidPlane

args.name: Dir1Angle
args.doc: Angle in radians of revolution in direction 1; only applies if Dir1Type is swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind

args.name: Dir2Angle
args.doc: Angle in radians of revolution in direction 2; only applies if Dir2Type is swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind

args.name: OffsetReverse1
args.doc: True to reverse the offset direction in direction 1, false to not; only applies if Dir1Type is swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: OffsetReverse2
args.doc: True to reverse the offset direction in direction 2, false to not; only applies if Dir2Type is swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: OffsetDistance1
args.doc: Offset distance in direction 1; only applies if Dir1Type is swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: OffsetDistance2
args.doc: Offset distance in direction 2; only applies if Dir2Type is swEndConditions_e.swEndCondOffsetFromSurface

args.name: ThinType
args.doc: Type and direction as defined in swThinWallType_e

args.name: ThinThickness1
args.doc: Wall thickness in direction 1 (if ThinType is swThinWallType_e.swThinWallMidPlane, (ThinThickness1)/2 is used for each direction)

args.name: ThinThickness2
args.doc: Wall thickness in direction 2 (only applies if ThinType is swThinWallType_e.swThinWallTwoDirection)

args.name: Merge
args.doc: True to merge the results into a multi-body part, false to not

args.name: UseFeatScope
args.doc: True if the feature only affects selected bodies, false if the feature affects all bodies (see Remarks)

args.name: UseAutoSelect
args.doc: True to automatically select all bodies and have the feature affect those bodies, false to select the bodies or components that the feature affects (see Remarks)

args.name: Options
args.doc: Options as defined by swFeatureChamferOption_e

args.name: ChamferType
args.doc: Chamfer type as defined by swChamferType_e

args.name: Width
args.doc: If ChamferType is swChamferAngleDistance, then specify width of chamfer

args.name: Angle
args.doc: If ChamferType is swChamferAngleDistance, then specify the angle of the }}-->}}-->chamfer

args.name: OtherDist
args.doc: If ChamferType is swChamferEqualDistance, then you can specify a single value so that all distances are equal

args.name: VertexChamDist1
args.doc: If ChamferType is swChamferDistanceDistance or swChamferVertex, then specify a value for the distance on first side

args.name: VertexChamDist2
args.doc: If ChamferType is swChamferDistanceDistance or swChamferVertex, then specify a value for the distance on second side

args.name: VertexChamDist3
args.doc: If ChamferType is swChamferVertex, then specify a value for the distance on third side

IFeatureManager.InsertMirrorFeature2.summary: Mirrors selected features, faces, bodies, and structure systems about a selected plane or planar face.

args.name: BMirrorBody
args.doc: True to mirror solid bodies; false to mirror a feature or face

args.name: BGeometryPattern
args.doc: True to mirror only the feature geometry, false to solve the entire feature; applies to mirroring features only

args.name: BMerge
args.doc: True to merge any mirrored solid bodies, false to not; applies to mirroring solid bodies only

args.name: BKnit
args.doc: True to knit surfaces, false to not; applies to mirroring surfaces only

args.name: ScopeOptions
args.doc: Feature scope as defined by swFeatureScope_e

args.name: Dia
args.doc: Hole diameter

args.name: Sd
args.doc: True for single-ended, false for double-ended

args.name: Flip
args.doc: True to flip the direction to cut, false to not

args.name: Dir
args.doc: True to flip direction to extrude, false to not

args.name: T1
args.doc: Termination type for first end as defined in swEndConditions_e

args.name: T2
args.doc: Termination type for second end as defined in swEndConditions_e

args.name: D1
args.doc: Depth of extrusion for first end in meters

args.name: D2
args.doc: Depth of extrusion for second end in meters

args.name: Dchk1
args.doc: True allows draft angle in first direction, false does not allow drafting

args.name: Dchk2
args.doc: True allows draft angle in second direction, false does not allow drafting

args.name: Ddir1
args.doc: For first draft angle to be inward use true, for draft angle outward use false

args.name: Ddir2
args.doc: For second draft angle to be inward use true, for draft angle outward use false

args.name: Dang1
args.doc: Draft angle for first end

args.name: Dang2
args.doc: Draft angle for second end

args.name: OffsetReverse1
args.doc: If you chose to offset the first end condition from another face or plane, then true specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in a direction toward the sketch

args.name: OffsetReverse2
args.doc: If you chose to offset the second end condition from another face or plane, then true specifies offset in direction away from the sketch, false specifies offset from the face or plane in a direction toward the sketch

args.name: TranslateSurface1
args.doc: True to use an offset relative to the surface or the plane selected, false to use a true offset

args.name: TranslateSurface2
args.doc: True to use an offset relative to the surface or the plane selected, false to use a true offset

args.name: UseFeatScope
args.doc: True if the feature only affects selected bodies, false if the feature affects all bodies

args.name: UseAutoSelect
args.doc: True to automatically select all bodies and have the feature affect those bodies, false to select the bodies the feature affects

args.name: AssemblyFeatureScope
args.doc: True if the assembly feature only affects selected components in the assembly, false if the assembly feature affects all components in the assembly

args.name: AutoSelectComponents
args.doc: True to auto-select all affected components, false to not (use the selected components only)

args.name: PropagateFeatureToParts
args.doc: True to propagate the assembly feature to the components in the model that it affects, false to not

IGearMateFeatureData.EntitiesToMate.summary: Gets or sets the entities to mate in this gear mate.

IGearMateFeatureData.GearRatioDenominato.summary: Gets or sets the denominator of the gear ratio of this gear mate.

IGearMateFeatureData.GearRatioNumerator.summary: Gets or sets the numerator of the gear ratio of this gear mate.

IGearMateFeatureData.Reverse.summary: Gets or sets whether to change the direction of rotation of the gears relative to one another in this gear mate.

IModelDoc2.AddDimension2.summary: Creates a display dimension at the specified location for selected entities.

args.name: X
args.doc: Dimension text location in meters

args.name: Y
args.doc: Dimension text location in meters

args.name: Z
args.doc: Dimension text location in meters

args.name: All
args.doc: True clears the entire existing selection list, false clears only the items in the active selection list (see Remarks)

IModelDoc2.EditRebuild3.summary: Rebuilds only those features that need to be rebuilt in the active configuration in the model.

IModelDoc2.EditUndo2.summary: Undoes the specified number of actions in the active SOLIDWORKS session.

args.name: Steps
args.doc: Number of actions to undo

IModelDoc2.FeatureByPositionReverse.summary: Gets the nth from last feature in the document.

args.name: Num
args.doc: Number of feature from the last feature in the FeatureManager design tree; 0 is the last feature in FeatureManager design tree

IModelDoc2.GetFeatureCount.summary: Gets the number of features in this document.

args.name: StringIn
args.doc: Name of parameter (for example, "D1@Base-Revolve")

IModelDoc2.SaveBMP.summary: Saves the current view as a bitmap (BMP) file.

args.name: FileNameIn
args.doc: Path and file name of the new BMP file

args.name: WidthIn
args.doc: Width of the BMP

args.name: HeightIn
args.doc: Height of the BMP

args.name: Name
args.doc: Name of object to select or an empty string

args.name: Type
args.doc: Type of object (uppercase) as defined in swSelectType_e or an empty string

args.name: X
args.doc: X selection location or 0

args.name: Y
args.doc: Y selection location or 0

args.name: Z
args.doc: Z selection location or 0

args.name: Append
args.doc: If... An, if entity is... Then... True Not already selected Entity is appended to the current selection list Already selected Entity is removed from the current selection list False Not already selected Current selection is cleared and then the entity is put on the list Already selected Current selection list remains the same

args.name: Mark
args.doc: Value that you want to use as a mark; this value is used by other functions that require ordered selection (see Remarks)

args.name: Callout

args.name: TopDoc

args.name: Component
args.doc: Component in which the feature is to be modified

ISweepFeatureData.AdvancedSmoothing.summary: Gets or sets whether to apply advanced smoothing to this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.AlignWithEndFaces.summary: Gets or sets whether to align this sweep with the end faces.

ISweepFeatureData.AssemblyFeatureScope.summary: Gets and sets whether this swept-cut feature affects only selected components in the assembly.

ISweepFeatureData.AutoSelect.summary: Gets or sets whether to automatically select all bodies in a multibody part to be affected by this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.AutoSelectComponents.summary: Gets and sets whether to automatically select all assembly components to be affected by this swept-cutfeature.

ISweepFeatureData.CircularProfile.summary: Gets or sets whether to use a circular profile for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.CircularProfileDiamete.summary: Gets or sets the diameter of the circular profile for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.D1ReverseTwistDir.summary: Gets or sets whether to reverse the twist of this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.D2ReverseTwistDir.summary: Gets or sets whether to reverse the twist in Direction 2 of this bidirectional sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.Direction.summary: Gets or sets the direction type of this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.EndDirectionVector.summary: Obsolete. Gets or sets the direction vector in which to end this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.EndTangencyType.summary: Gets or sets tangency at the end of the sweep path of this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.FeatureScope.summary: Gets or sets whether to use scope in a multibody part for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.FeatureScopeBodies.summary: Gets or sets the solid bodies in a multibody part affected by this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.GetCutSweepOption.summary: Gets the type of this cut sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.GetD2TwistAngle.summary: Gets the twist angle in Direction 2 of this bidirectional sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.GetFeatureScopeBodiesC.summary: Gets the number of solid bodies affected by the sweep feature in a multibody part.

ISweepFeatureData.GetGuideCurvesCount.summary: Gets a number of guide curves for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.GetGuideCurvesType.summary: Gets the type of guide curves in this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.GetPathAlignmentDirect.summary: Gets the direction vector of the specified type for this sweep feature.

args.name: Type
args.doc: Type of direction vector as defined in swSelectType_e: swSelDATAMAXES (axis) swSelDATUMPLANES (plane) swSelEDGES (edge) swSelFACES (face)

ISweepFeatureData.GetPathType.summary: Gets the path type for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.GetProfileType.summary: Gets the profile type of this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.GetTwistAngle.summary: Gets the angle at which to twist this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.GetWallThickness.summary: Gets the wall thickness, forward (Direction 1) or reverse (Direction 2), of this thin-walled sweep feature.

args.name: Forward
args.doc: True for Direction 1, false for Direction 2

ISweepFeatureData.GuideCurves.summary: Gets or sets the guide curves for this sweep feature.

args.name: TopDoc
args.doc: Top-level document

args.name: Component
args.doc: Component in which the feature is to be modified

ISweepFeatureData.IsBossFeature.summary: Gets whether the sweep feature is a boss feature.

ISweepFeatureData.IsThinFeature.summary: Gets whether this is a thin-walled sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.MaintainTangency.summary: Gets or sets whether to merge tangent faces in this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.Merge.summary: Gets or sets whether to merge the results of this swept-boss feature for a multibody part.

ISweepFeatureData.MergeSmoothFaces.summary: Gets or sets whether to merge the smooth faces of this sweep feature that uses guide curves.

ISweepFeatureData.Path.summary: Gets or sets the sweep path for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.PathAlignmentType.summary: Gets or sets the alignment of the sweep path in this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.Profile.summary: Gets and sets the sketch profile or tool body for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.PropagateFeatureToPart.summary: Gets and sets whether to extend the swept-cut feature to all affected parts in the assembly.

ISweepFeatureData.ReleaseSelectionAccess.summary: Releases access to the selections for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.SetD2TwistAngle.summary: Sets the twist angle in Direction 2 of this bidirectional sweep feature.

args.doc: Angle of twist in radians in Direction 2

ISweepFeatureData.SetPathAlignmentDirect.summary: Sets the direction vector for path alignment in this sweep feature.

args.name: Dir
args.doc: Plane, planar face, line, edge, cylinder, axis, or a pair of vertices that defines the direction

ISweepFeatureData.SetTwistAngle.summary: Sets the angle at which to twist this sweep feature.

args.name: Angle
args.doc: Angle of twist in radians

args.name: WallThickness
args.doc: Wall thickness

ISweepFeatureData.StartTangencyType.summary: Gets and sets the tangency at the start of the sweep path for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.SweepType.summary: Gets the type of this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.TangentPropagation.summary: Gets or sets whether to propagate the sweep to the next tangent edge for this sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.ThinFeature.summary: Gets or sets whether to make this sweep feature a thin-walled feature.

ISweepFeatureData.ThinWallType.summary: Gets or sets the type of this thin-walled sweep feature.

ISweepFeatureData.TwistControlType.summary: Gets or sets the type of twist control for this sweep feature.

values.doc: Base ordinate and its subordinates are of this type

swSelectType_e.summary: Values for types of returned IDs.

values.name: swFeatureScope_AllBodies
values.doc: All of the bodies in the multibody part are affected by the Mirror feature.

values.name: swFeatureScope_SelectedBodiesWithAutoSelect
values.doc: Only the specified bodies in the multibody part are affected by the Mirror feature when AutoSelect is true.

values.name: swFeatureScope_SelectedBodiesWithOutAutoSelect
values.doc: Only the specified bodies in the multibody part are affected by the Mirror feature when AutoSelect is false.
